pokemon.name: Porygon
pokemon.description: A POKéMON that consists entirely of programming code. Capable of moving freely in cyberspace.

models.profile: SymPy is not an LLM but a pure-Python computer-algebra library that deterministically manipulates symbols to emit template proofs. Porygon, the first wholly artificial Pokémon made entirely of programming code, mirrors this engineered, code-native, rule-driven artifact.

pokemon.name: Metagross
pokemon.description: METAGROSS has four brains in total. Combined, the four brains can breeze through difficult calculations faster than a supercomputer. This POKéMON can float in the air by tucking in its four legs.

models.profile: `decide` is not a model but a Lean 4 core tactic that proves decidable propositions by brute-force kernel computation, reducing a goal to a definitive true/false verdict. Metagross fits: its four-brain neural network outpaces a supercomputer, crunching data into one decisive, deterministic answer.

pokemon.name: Unown
pokemon.description: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

models.profile: Unown's name literally reads as "unknown," matching an OpenRouter request whose underlying model is unidentified. Like Unown's 28 interchangeable forms, it can resolve to any of many models behind one hidden identity.
